title: gilia septentrionalis
intro: Gilia septentrionalis, also known as the Northern Gilia, is a flowering plant species in the plantain family (Plantaginaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of western North America and is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. Gilia septentrionalis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, promoting digestion, and supporting immune function.
